findings.problem: Sanitization drift at the chapter's peak rupture: the prose pulls back into summary/montage (crowd noise, social-media optics) instead of fully rendering the immediate family collision it has set up, reducing embodied consequence right when specificity is most needed.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rework the immediate aftermath around snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:208, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:218, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:230, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:306 so at least one sustained on-page confrontation occurs between Marisol and either Gloria or Carina before the media beat; replace generalized crowd-summary lines with concrete actions, quoted speech, and physical responses tied to named characters.
findings.acceptance_test: PASS if the revised passage between snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:208 and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:230 contains a direct Marisol-vs-family exchange of at least 3 consecutive dialogue/action beats with specific bodily detail, and Gloria's departure at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:306 is preceded by an explicit interpersonal break on page; FAIL if the climax remains primarily summarized as crowd noise or abstract split.

findings.problem: Causal priority blurs after the poem: extended press handling takes foreground while the core dramatic line (family rupture and Marisol's immediate consequential choice) is deferred, softening momentum from act to cost.
findings.rewrite_direction: Tighten or interleave the press sequence at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:248, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:272, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:312 with a decisive immediate-action beat from Marisol tied to family fallout (for example: pursuit/refusal/call-for-help boundary) before the headline lands.
findings.acceptance_test: PASS if, before or by snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:312, Marisol makes one irreversible in-scene choice that directly responds to family rupture and changes the next-scene trajectory; FAIL if the sequence remains mostly reactive Q&A plus headline escalation without a decisive action beat.

findings.problem: Crowd-voice exchange reads as interchangeable slogan lines; register and rhythm do not sufficiently differentiate church loyalists, younger arts voices, and bystander moral framing, weakening idiolect separation under pressure.
findings.rewrite_direction: Revise the crowd argument around snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:236 through snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:242 so at least two speakers carry distinct sociolect markers (church idiom vs arts/media jargon vs neighborhood colloquial), including interruption texture and contraction-heavy spoken rhythm where appropriate.
findings.acceptance_test: PASS if at least two crowd lines in the revised snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:236-242 block are unmistakably attributable to different social positions by diction and cadence (not interchangeable when swapped); FAIL if the lines remain generic moral binaries with near-identical syntax.

findings.problem: The repeated 'as if ...' explanatory comparison appears more than twice and starts to feel like an autopilot sentence shape rather than a deliberate stylistic choice, flattening line-level variety.
findings.rewrite_direction: At snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:7, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:73, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:230, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:306, replace at least three 'as if' constructions with varied devices (direct concrete observation, metaphor without hedge framing, or interior free-indirect inference).
findings.acceptance_test: PASS if the revised cited block retains no more than two total 'as if' constructions and each replacement still delivers a concrete, scene-specific image; FAIL if four or more 'as if' comparisons remain in chapter_16 or replacements become generic abstractions.

findings.problem: Interiority drop: after the poem lands, the focalizer runs through a long external-only stretch (10+ consecutive short paragraphs) before re-entering Marisol's internal state, briefly turning the chapter into a transcripted crowd scene.
findings.rewrite_direction: Inject Marisol interior beats into the aftermath block beginning at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:208 and before snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:244, using thought/sensation/self-assessment tied to specific external triggers so escalation remains psychologically anchored.
findings.acceptance_test: PASS if the revised span from snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:208 to snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:244 includes at least three explicit internal-experience sentences from Marisol and never exceeds five consecutive paragraphs without interiority; FAIL if the block still runs as mostly external crowd coverage.

summary: Chapter 16 strongly satisfies continuity, canonical tense (past), and core state-delta requirements, with generally consistent principal-character voices (Marisol/Gloria/Carina/Nora). However, unresolved craft and prose issues remain: a HIGH sanitization-by-summary pullback at the dramatic apex, a momentum dip in post-poem causal sequencing, crowd-voice interchangeability, repeated comparison templates, and a 10+ paragraph interiority drought in the immediate aftermath. On fail-by-default standards, all four lenses remain FAIL until those passages are revised.
